# Contributing to dijidaro
We welcome contributions from anyone who is interested in improving dijidaro. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just getting started, there are many ways that you can help us make our web app better.
The following is a set of guidelines for contributing to dijidaro. Use your best judgement and feel free to propose changes to this document in a pull request.

### Table of Contents

[Code of Conduct](#code-of-conduct)

[What should I know before I get started?](#what-should-i-know-before-i-get-started)

[How Can I Contribute?](#how-can-i-contribute)
  * [Reporting Bugs](#reporting-bugs)
  * [Pull Requests](#pull-requests)

[Code Reviews](#code-reviews)

[Styleguides](#styleguides)
  * [Git Commit Messages](#git-commit-messages)

## Code of conduct
This project and everyone participating in it is governed by the [dijidaro Code of Conduct](.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you are expected to uphold this code. Please report unacceptable behavior to [dijidaro@github.com](mailto:dijidaro@github.com).

## What should I know before I get started?
- Pending

## How Can I Contribute?

### Reporting Bugs

This section guides you through submitting a bug report for dijidaro. Following these guidelines helps maintainers and the community understand your report :pencil:, reproduce the behavior :computer: :computer:, and find related reports :mag_right:.
Before creating bug reports, please check [this list](#before-submitting-a-bug-report) as you might find out that you don't need to create one. When you are creating a bug report, please [include as many details as possible](#how-do-i-submit-a-good-bug-report). Fill out [the required template](https://github.com/dijidaro/dijidaro/tree/main/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/BUG_REPORT.yml), the information it asks for helps us resolve issues faster.

> **Note:** If you find a **Closed** issue that seems like it is the same thing that you're experiencing, open a new issue and include a link to the original issue in the body of your new one.

### Pull Requests

The process described here has several goals:

- Maintain dijidaro's quality
- Fix problems that are important to users
- Enable a sustainable system for dijidaro's maintainers to review contributions

Please follow these steps to have your contribution considered by the maintainers:
1. Follow all instructions in [the template](.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md)
2. Follow the [styleguides](#styleguides)

## Code Reviews
All pull requests will be reviewed by a maintainer. The maintainer may request changes or ask for clarifications. Please respond to all review comments in a timely manner.

## Styleguides

### Git Commit Messages
* Use the present tense ("Add feature" not "Added feature")
* Use the imperative mood ("Move cursor to..." not "Moves cursor to...")
* Consider starting the commit message with an applicable emoji:
  * :bug: `:bug:` when fixing a bug
  * :art: `:art:` when improving the format/structure of the code
  * :racehorse: `:racehorse:` when improving performance
  * :memo: `:memo:` when writing docs
  * :fire: `:fire:` when removing code or files
  * :green_heart: `:green_heart:` when fixing the CI build
  * :white_check_mark: `:white_check_mark:` when adding tests
  * :lock: `:lock:` when dealing with security
  * :arrow_up: `:arrow_up:` when upgrading dependencies

Thank you

We appreciate all contributions to dijidaro. Thank you for helping us make our web app better!
